title: cereus alatus
intro: Cereus alatus, also known as the 'Buddha's hand', is a rare and unique variety of the citron plant (Citrus medica), characterized by its finger-like branches that lack thorns. The medicinal properties of Cereus alatus include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve nausea, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of consuming Cereus alatus include aiding in weight loss, reducing inflammation, and providing antioxidant properties, making it a popular ingredient in traditional Chinese medicine. The herbal preparations of Cereus alatus typically involve drying and powdering the fruit, which can then be used in teas, tinctures, and other remedies.
